name: ExtensionPermission_Justification
title: R5: The asserted justification for using the data (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Permission.justification` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Permission.justification` 0..1 `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `Permission.justification` 0..1 `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Permission.justification` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Permission` is not mapped.
Element `Permission.justification` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: The regulatory grounds upon which this Permission builds
element.definition: This would be a codeableconcept, or a coding, which can be constrained to , for example, the 6 grounds for processing in GDPR.

element.comment:
Element `Permission.justification.evidence` is part of an existing definition because parent element `Permission.justification` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `Permission.justification.evidence` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Permission` is not mapped.
Element `Permission.justification.evidence` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
While any resource may be used, DocumentReference, Consent, PlanDefinition, and Contract would be most frequent
